prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
# Chronicles of the Quarterly Parchment: Q3 Results

Verily, the mystical "cloud" which houses our lord's software hath expanded its dominion to seven-and-forty new kingdoms, though I confess I know not how vapor might contain such sorcerous calculations. The revenue streams have flowed most bountifully like the River Tiber in springtime, increasing by two-and-twenty percent, though I remain perplexed as to why gold coins transform into recurring "subscriptions" that vanish into the ether. Our noble CEO Sir William of Gates-Not-Actually spoke of "scalable solutions" and "customer acquisition costs," terms surely referring to some manner of weighing merchants at the marketplace before purchase. The company's "burn rate" concerns me greatly, as I had not realized our ledgers were being set aflame monthly, yet the shareholders appear most pleased by this regular immolation of treasury.
